name: graph-wrapper

metadata.name: graph-wrapper
metadata.summary: Wrappers of popular network libraries with type annotation

documentation:
# graph ![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/graph-wrapper)

Wrapper of popular network libraries with type annotation

functions.name: graph.retworkx.api.digraph_all_simple_paths
documentation:
Return all simple paths between 2 nodes in a PyDiGraph object
A simple path is a path with no repeated nodes.

Args:
    g: The graph to find the path in
    source: The node index to find the paths from
    target: The node index to find the paths to
    min_depth: The minimum depth of the path to include in the output
        list of paths. By default all paths are included regardless of depth,
        set to None will behave like the default.
    cutoff: The maximum depth of path (number of edges) to include in the output list
        of paths. By default includes all paths regardless of depth, setting to
        None will behave like default.

Return a list of lists where each inner list is a path containing edges

functions.name: graph.retworkx.api.dag_longest_path
documentation:
Return the longest path in a DAG

Args:
    g: The graph to find the longest path in

Return a list of nodes of the longest path in DAG

functions.name: graph.retworkx.api.is_weakly_connected
documentation:
Return True if the graph is weakly connected. Raise NullGraph if an empty graph is passed in
Args:
    g: The graph to check

functions.name: graph.retworkx.api.weakly_connected_components
documentation:
Return the weakly connected components of the graph

Args:
    g: The graph to check

Return a list of lists where each inner list is a weakly connected component

functions.name: graph.retworkx.api.has_cycle
documentation: Test if graph has cycle

functions.name: graph.retworkx.api.digraph_find_cycle
documentation:
Return the first cycle encountered during DFS of a given PyDiGraph from a node, empty list is returned if no cycle is found.

Args:
    g: The graph to find the cycle in
    source: node id to find a cycle for

functions.name: graph.retworkx.api.topological_sort
documentation:
Return a list of node ids in topological sort order. The first node will have no incoming edges and the last node will
have no outgoing edges if the graph is a DAG. In order words, a node will appear before any nodes it has edges to.

Args:
    g: The graph to find the topological sort for

functions.name: graph.viz.texttree.texttree
documentation: Print the graph to the environment if possible. When env is browser, users have to print it manually

functions.name: graph.viz.graphviz.draw
documentation:
Args:
    graph: the graph to draw
    node_label: the label of a node
    edge_label: the label of an edge
    node_styles: the styles of a node
    edge_styles: the styles of an edge
    filename : str | none
        output to a file or display immediately (inline if this is jupyter lab)
    format: png | jpg
        image format
    quality: int
        if it's < 100, we will compress the image using PIL
    no_display: bool
        if the code is running inside Jupyter, if enable, it returns the object and manually display (default is
        automatically display)
    line_width: int
        every line is at most width characters long
    max_lines: int
        text  will contain at most max_lines lines, with placeholder appearing at the end of the output.

functions.name: graph.viz.graphviz.draw_auto
documentation:
Draw the graph into a directory, naming incrementally for debugging.

This function is not thread-safe.

methods.name: graph.retworkx.str_digraph.RetworkXStrDiGraph._sync_idmap
documentation: Update idmap with the current graph

A directed graph implementation using retworkx.

Each edge is uniquely identified by its id or a triple of (source, target, key)

Args:
    check_cycle (bool, optional): whether to check cycle during insertion or updating. Defaults to False.
    multigraph (bool, optional): whether allowing parallel edges. Defaults to True. When False if a method call is made that would add parallel edges the the weight/weight from that method call will be used to update the existing edge in place

methods.name: graph.retworkx.digraph._RetworkXDiGraph.find_node
documentation:
Find node in the graph that is equal (`==`) given a specific weight.

The `__eq__` method of value is going to be used to compare the nodes.

This algorithm has a worst case of O(n) since it searches the node indices in order.
If there is more than one node in the graph with the same weight only the first match (by node index) will be returned.

methods.name: graph.retworkx.digraph._RetworkXDiGraph.__eq__
documentation: Check if content of two graphs are equal

A directed graph implementation using retworkx.

Each edge is uniquely identified by its id or a triple of (source, target, key)

Args:
    check_cycle (bool, optional): whether to check cycle during insertion or updating. Defaults to False.
    multigraph (bool, optional): whether allowing parallel edges. Defaults to True. When False if a method call is made that would add parallel edges the the weight/weight from that method call will be used to update the existing edge in place

methods.name: graph.interface.IGraph.copy
documentation: Create a shallow copy of the graph

methods.name: graph.interface.IGraph.check_integrity
documentation: Check if ids/refs in the graph are consistent

Represent a graph. The graph is directed by default, but for undirected graph, methods won't take into
account the direction of the edges. For example, `successors` and `predecessors` returns the same results
